title: The Deepwater Navigation Silence

features.name: chart dissolution
features.desc-on-touch: Lines dissolve into the stone beneath. The map and the reef are becoming one argument.

features.name: pressure chamber
features.desc-on-touch: The weight of water everywhere. No sound, no echo. Silence becomes a physical thing down here.

features.name: memory stone
features.desc-on-touch: Salt-crusted granite holding the shapes of a thousand soundings. The stone remembers more than any chart.

features.name: impossible passage
features.desc-on-touch: A route that charts say should not exist. Your hand finds it anyway. The water knows paths that maps abandoned.

features.name: deepwater listening
features.desc-on-touch: A structure that listens to the ocean floor. It has been recording for longer than charts have been made.

reverie.title: REVERIE — the deep place where sound goes to age
narr: She waded into the channel at dusk, tracing the floor
with her foot. You followed. The water rose to your chest.
"The charts can't touch here," she said. "Below them."
You both stood still. The weight of the water was actual.
She took your hand. Her grip was steady. The maps above
meant nothing. Down here, only the stone knew the way.

inscription: when charts dissolve, the stone and the navigator must learn to read each other

file.name: Deepwater_Soundings_Uncharted.mp3
file.note: Hydrophone recordings from below the mapped channels. The archive cannot determine where these recordings end and the archive's own listening begins.

exits.label: back toward the reefs that learned language
deeper, where trench walls remember their forming
toward shallows where the light gives up
